title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Peatlands are a type of wetland that covers only three percent of the Earth’s land surface, (0) yet they store more carbon than all the world’s forests combined. (9) Although they are often overlooked in climate discussions, their preservation is vital for meeting global emission targets. (10) Such is the efficiency of peat in sequestering carbon that damaging these ecosystems releases massive amounts of greenhouse gases. (11) Provided that these areas remain waterlogged, the organic matter they contain does not fully decompose, keeping carbon trapped underground. (12) Not only do peatlands regulate water cycles, but they also provide a unique habitat for rare and endangered species. (13) Regardless of their ecological importance, many peatlands are still being drained for agriculture and fuel extraction today. (14) Not until recently did the international community begin to implement large-scale restoration projects to protect these vulnerable landscapes. (15) In spite of the challenges, rewetting degraded bogs has proven to be a highly effective way to mitigate climate change. (16) Whether we can protect the remaining peatlands from further destruction will be a decisive factor in our future environmental stability. These ancient ecosystems are a critical natural defense against the warming of our global atmosphere.

example.text: Peatlands cover only three percent of the Earth’s land surface, (0) yet they store more carbon than all the world’s forests combined.
example.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a surprising or contrasting fact.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis before a noun phrase.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did the... community begin').
Part of the prepositional linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction introducing a doubt or two possibilities.
